steps.title: Convert percentage statements to ratios
steps.content:
From 27% of h is equivalent to 24% of j, write $0.27h = 0.24j$. Multiply by 100 to clear decimals: $27h = 24j$. This gives us the ratio: $$\frac{h}{j} = \frac{24}{27} = \frac{8}{9}$$

From j is equivalent to 45% of k: $$\frac{j}{k} = \frac{45}{100} = \frac{9}{20}$$

steps.title: Chain the ratios to find h in terms of k
steps.content:
Multiply the two ratios to relate $h$ directly to $k$: $$\frac{h}{k} = \frac{h}{j} \cdot \frac{j}{k} = \frac{8}{9} \cdot \frac{9}{20} = \frac{72}{180} = \frac{2}{5}$$

Therefore $h = \frac{2}{5}k$.
